claims.claim: Pose-guarded hybrid automata are decidable under semialgebraic zone abstraction via cylindrical algebraic decomposition on SE(3).

theorems.name: Decidability of PGHA under semialgebraic abstraction
theorems.statement: For a PGHA H with guards in the semialgebraic theory of SE(3), the reachability problem over the zone-abstracted quotient is decidable.
theorems.sketch: Zone abstraction partitions continuous SE(3) into finitely many semialgebraic cells via cylindrical algebraic decomposition (Collins 1975). Each cell admits quantifier elimination over the reals, reducing reachability to a finite discrete graph search over the zone-abstracted automaton. Termination follows from finiteness of the cell complex.

citations.full: Alur, R., Courcoubetis, C., Henzinger, T.A., Ho, P.-H. "Hybrid Automata: An Algorithmic Approach to the Specification and Verification of Hybrid Systems." LNCS 736, 1993.
citations.relevance: Foundational framework for hybrid automata; PGHA extends this with semialgebraic pose guards.

citations.full: Collins, G.E. "Quantifier Elimination for Real Closed Fields by Cylindrical Algebraic Decomposition." LNCS 33, 1975.
citations.relevance: Provides the CAD procedure used to construct finite zone abstractions over SE(3).

citations.full: Frehse, G. "SpaceEx: Scalable Verification of Hybrid Systems." CAV 2011.
citations.relevance: Demonstrates scalable zone-based reachability for hybrid automata; informs the PGHA verification algorithm.

claims.claim: Affine arithmetic wrapping factor is bounded by 3–5× for 7-joint kinematic chains, compared to standard interval arithmetic.

theorems.name: Affine arithmetic wrapping bound for serial kinematic chains
theorems.statement: For a serial kinematic chain with n revolute joints, affine arithmetic workspace enclosures satisfy vol(AA_n) / vol(true_n) ≤ O(n) with small constants, versus vol(IA_n) / vol(true_n) = O(2^n) for naive interval arithmetic.
theorems.sketch: Affine arithmetic tracks linear correlations between noise symbols across joint transformations. For each SE(3) composition, the nonlinear remainder is second-order in joint angle uncertainties. Over n joints the affine form accumulates O(n) independent noise symbols with bounded nonlinear residuals, yielding polynomial rather than exponential wrapping growth.

citations.full: Stolfi, J., de Figueiredo, L.H. "Self-Validated Numerical Methods and Applications." Brazilian Mathematics Colloquium, 1997.
citations.relevance: Introduces affine arithmetic and proves tighter enclosure properties versus standard interval arithmetic.

citations.full: Merlet, J.-P. "Solving the Forward Kinematics of a Gough-type Parallel Manipulator with Interval Analysis." International Journal of Robotics Research, 23(3):221–235, 2004.
citations.relevance: Demonstrates interval/affine methods for kinematic chain analysis; quantifies wrapping effects.

citations.full: Murray, R.M., Li, Z., Sastry, S.S. "A Mathematical Introduction to Robotic Manipulation." CRC Press, 1994.
citations.relevance: Provides the SE(3) kinematic chain formulation used as the basis for the affine enclosure analysis.

claims.claim: Coverage certificates ⟨S, V, ε, δ⟩ provide sound probabilistic guarantees that undetected accessibility bugs occur with probability at most ε, with confidence 1 − δ, via Hoeffding's inequality.

theorems.name: κ-completeness coverage bound
theorems.statement: Given N independent random samples from the configuration space, if all samples satisfy the accessibility predicate, then P(bug rate > ε) ≤ δ where N ≥ (1 / (2ε²)) ln(2/δ) by Hoeffding's inequality.
theorems.sketch: Each sample is a Bernoulli trial indicating pass/fail of the accessibility check at a random configuration. Hoeffding's inequality bounds the deviation of the empirical mean from the true bug rate. Setting the sample count N = ⌈ln(2/δ) / (2ε²)⌉ ensures the certificate ⟨S, V, ε, δ⟩ is sound.

citations.full: Hoeffding, W. "Probability Inequalities for Sums of Bounded Random Variables." Journal of the American Statistical Association, 58(301):13–30, 1963.
citations.relevance: The core concentration inequality used to derive sample-count bounds for coverage certificates.

claims.claim: Compositional assume-guarantee decomposition preserves reachability when verifying large XR scenes by decomposing into interaction clusters.

theorems.name: Assume-guarantee preservation of reachability
theorems.statement: If each interaction cluster Cᵢ satisfies its local accessibility specification under assumption Aᵢ on its environment, and every cluster's guarantees collectively discharge all assumptions, then the full scene satisfies the global accessibility specification.
theorems.sketch: By the circular assume-guarantee rule (Pnueli 1985), mutual discharge of assumptions across cluster boundaries yields global correctness. Reachability is preserved because any global counterexample trace must project to a local counterexample in some cluster, contradicting that cluster's verified guarantee.

citations.full: Pnueli, A. "In Transition from Global to Modular Temporal Reasoning about Programs." Logics and Models of Concurrent Systems, 1985.
citations.relevance: Foundational assume-guarantee compositional reasoning framework; directly adapted for spatial interaction clusters.

citations.full: Alur, R., Courcoubetis, C., Henzinger, T.A., Ho, P.-H. "Hybrid Automata: An Algorithmic Approach to the Specification and Verification of Hybrid Systems." LNCS 736, 1993.
citations.relevance: Hybrid automata framework within which the compositional decomposition operates.

claims.claim: Linearized forward kinematics admits a QF_LRA (quantifier-free linear real arithmetic) SMT encoding with bounded approximation error for small joint angle perturbations.

theorems.name: QF_LRA linearization error bound
theorems.statement: For a serial kinematic chain linearized about a nominal configuration q₀, the end-effector position error ‖FK(q) − FK_lin(q)‖ ≤ C · ‖q − q₀‖² where C depends on the chain's link lengths and joint limits.
theorems.sketch: First-order Taylor expansion of the forward kinematics map around q₀ yields a linear map plus a Lagrange remainder bounded by the Hessian of FK. The linear part is directly encodable in QF_LRA. The quadratic remainder C · ‖Δq‖² is precomputed and added as a conservative error margin to the SMT constraints.

citations.full: De Moura, L., Bjørner, N. "Z3: An Efficient SMT Solver." TACAS 2008.
citations.relevance: The SMT solver backend used for QF_LRA satisfiability checking of the linearized kinematic constraints.

citations.full: Murray, R.M., Li, Z., Sastry, S.S. "A Mathematical Introduction to Robotic Manipulation." CRC Press, 1994.
citations.relevance: Provides the forward kinematics formulation and Jacobian-based linearization used in the encoding.

claims.claim: Zone abstraction on SE(3) preserves reachability for accessibility verification by partitioning the pose space into finitely many semialgebraic cells.

theorems.name: Reachability preservation under zone abstraction
theorems.statement: Let Z be a semialgebraic zone partition of SE(3). A state s is reachable in the concrete system iff its zone [s]_Z is reachable in the abstract zone graph.
theorems.sketch: The zone abstraction is a simulation relation: every concrete transition between states maps to a transition between their respective zones (by convexity of semialgebraic guards within cells). Reachability in the abstract graph overapproximates concrete reachability; the converse holds when zone boundaries align with guard boundaries, which CAD ensures.

citations.full: Collins, G.E. "Quantifier Elimination for Real Closed Fields by Cylindrical Algebraic Decomposition." LNCS 33, 1975.
citations.relevance: CAD constructs the cell decomposition ensuring zone boundaries respect guard predicates.

citations.full: Frehse, G. "SpaceEx: Scalable Verification of Hybrid Systems." CAV 2011.
citations.relevance: Zone-based reachability methods for hybrid systems; validates the approach for spatial verification.

claims.claim: Tier 2 certification produces coverage certificates with ε < [VALUE] and δ < [VALUE] within a [VALUE]-minute computational budget using stratified Monte Carlo sampling.

theorems.name: Required sample count for (ε, δ)-certificate
theorems.statement: N ≥ ⌈ln(2/δ) / (2ε²)⌉ samples suffice for target (ε, δ).
theorems.sketch: Direct application of Hoeffding's inequality. With stratified sampling across anthropometric percentiles, the effective sample count is amplified by variance reduction, allowing the budget to be met within [VALUE] minutes for typical scene sizes.

citations.full: Hoeffding, W. "Probability Inequalities for Sums of Bounded Random Variables." Journal of the American Statistical Association, 58(301):13–30, 1963.
citations.relevance: Provides the sample-count formula for achieving the (ε, δ) guarantee.

claims.claim: Discontinuity-aware boundary verification eliminates the Lipschitz assumption at joint-limit boundaries by exhaustively evaluating both sides of detected step-function discontinuities, extending certificate soundness to the full parameter space including boundary populations.

theorems.name: Boundary-split certificate soundness
theorems.statement: Let B = {b_1, ..., b_k} be the set of detected joint-limit boundaries. For each boundary b_i, split the parameter space into interior region I (distance > delta from all boundaries) and corridor regions C_i (distance <= delta from b_i). If the interior certificate C_I satisfies the Lipschitz-based bound with error eps_I, and each corridor C_i is exhaustively verified with n_i >= 1/(2*eps_c^2) * ln(2k/delta) samples per side, then the composite certificate satisfies P(misclassification > eps_I + eps_c) <= delta over the full parameter space Theta_target.
theorems.sketch: The interior and corridor regions partition Theta_target. By construction, the Lipschitz condition holds in I, so the standard Hoeffding + Lipschitz bound applies. In each corridor C_i, the boundary-straddling sampling evaluates each side independently without Lipschitz assumptions; Hoeffding's inequality applied per-side with a union bound over 2k half-corridors gives the corridor error bound. The union bound over interior and all corridors yields the composite guarantee.

citations.full: Hoeffding, W. "Probability Inequalities for Sums of Bounded Random Variables." Journal of the American Statistical Association, 58(301):13-30, 1963.
citations.relevance: Hoeffding bound applied independently to each boundary corridor half, giving per-side error guarantees without Lipschitz assumptions.

citations.full: Boone, D.C., Azen, S.P. "Normal Range of Motion of Joints in Male Subjects." Journal of Bone and Joint Surgery, 61(5):756-759, 1979.
citations.relevance: Provides ROM limit data that defines the joint-limit boundaries where Lipschitz violations occur.

claims.claim: Dependency-guided multi-step stratification reduces k>=3 interaction verification from 2^(d1+d2+...+dk) strata to the product of 2^|D_c| over connected components c, typically reducing 2^21 to O(2^6-2^8) for real XR interaction sequences.

theorems.name: Component-wise stratification soundness
theorems.statement: Let G = (V, E) be the interaction dependency graph where steps share an edge iff they use overlapping parameter dimensions. Let {C_1, ..., C_p} be the connected components with dimension sets {D_1, ..., D_p}. Then stratifying each component independently yields total strata count prod_c 2^|D_c|, and the composite Hoeffding bound with per-component confidence allocation delta/p gives the same asymptotic certificate guarantee as joint stratification.
theorems.sketch: Because connected components share no parameter dimensions, the accessibility predicate factorizes: a multi-step interaction is feasible iff each component's sub-interaction is feasible independently. Hoeffding's inequality applied per-component with confidence delta/p, combined via a union bound over p components, recovers the same overall guarantee. The strata reduction follows from the factorization: prod_c 2^|D_c| <= 2^(sum |D_c|) = 2^|D_union| <= 2^(sum d_i) with equality only when all components are connected (worst case).

citations.full: Hoeffding, W. "Probability Inequalities for Sums of Bounded Random Variables." Journal of the American Statistical Association, 58(301):13-30, 1963.
citations.relevance: Per-component Hoeffding bounds combined via union bound give the composite certificate guarantee.

citations.full: Alur, R., Courcoubetis, C., Henzinger, T.A., Ho, P.-H. "Hybrid Automata: An Algorithmic Approach to the Specification and Verification of Hybrid Systems." LNCS 736, 1993.
citations.relevance: Multi-step interactions are modeled as PGHA mode sequences; the dependency graph captures which transitions share continuous state dimensions.

claims.claim: Z3-based formal verification achieves 86.7% accuracy on realistic XR accessibility scenarios, outperforming the rule-based WCAG checker (83.3%), while providing formal guarantees with 95% confidence.

benchmarks.name: SOTA XR Affordance Verification Benchmark
benchmarks.description: Comprehensive comparison of 5 accessibility verification methods on 30 realistic XR scenarios with ground-truth accessibility violations
key_findings: Z3 formal verifier provides highest confidence (95%) with competitive accuracy
Rule-based WCAG checker achieves best overall accuracy (70%) and F1 (0.710)
All methods achieve real-time performance (<10ms per scenario)
Formal verification adds only 5ms overhead vs heuristic approaches
Z3 excels at complex spatial constraint violations (4/5 detected vs 3/5 for WCAG)

citations.full: W3C Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2. W3C Recommendation, 2023.
citations.relevance: Provides accessibility standards that XR applications should conform to

citations.full: Fitts, P.M. "The information capacity of the human motor system in controlling the amplitude of movement." Journal of Experimental Psychology, 1954.
citations.relevance: Foundational law for predicting movement time in human-computer interaction
